description: Duty drawback qualification for Forge. Identifies companies that import physical goods into the US AND export/ship internationally, making them eligible for duty drawback refunds.

categories.name: HIGH_CONFIDENCE
categories.description: Strong signals for BOTH import into US AND international shipping/export. Clear evidence of overseas manufacturing/sourcing AND ships outside US.

categories.name: MEDIUM_CONFIDENCE
categories.description: Likely qualifies but not all criteria explicitly confirmed. Strong signals for import OR export, with reasonable inference for the other based on industry.

categories.name: DISQUALIFIED
categories.description: Does not meet criteria: no physical products, confirmed no imports (US-made only), confirmed no international shipping, or is a pure retailer buying from US distributors.

products_found.description: 3-6 specific physical products found (empty if none)

import_signals.description: What signals suggest imports? e.g., 'Made in China', 'sourced from Vietnam', industry inference

export_signals.description: What signals suggest exports? e.g., 'ships worldwide', 'international shipping available'

questions.question: Does this company sell physical, tangible products that can be shipped?
questions.guidance: YES if: product catalogs, shop pages, add-to-cart buttons, product images showing shoes, apparel, electronics, tools, etc. NO if: pure services, software, consulting, digital products only.

questions.question: Does this company likely IMPORT goods into the United States?
questions.guidance: EXPLICIT SIGNALS: 'Made in China/Vietnam/Mexico', 'sourced from', 'manufactured overseas', 'imported'. INDUSTRY INFERENCE - almost always imported: Apparel/fashion brands (unless explicitly Made in USA with factory proof), Footwear/shoes, Electronics, Eyewear frames, German/Japanese/Swiss precision tools, Furniture (unless US factory mentioned), Toys, Sporting goods, Beauty/cosmetics, Jewelry. NEGATIVE SIGNALS: 'Made in USA', 'manufactured in our US facility', print-on-demand, local fabrication. NOTE: A retailer stocking brands is NOT the same as being an importer - they may buy from US distributors.

questions.question: Does this company ship/export goods OUTSIDE the United States?
questions.guidance: LOOK FOR: 'International shipping available', 'We ship worldwide', shipping page with country list, multi-currency checkout, currency/region selector. CHECK: shipping page, FAQ, footer, checkout. Canada/Mexico shipping counts as international.

questions.question: Is this company a BRAND OWNER (sells their own branded products) or DIRECT IMPORTER (imports products themselves)? Or are they just a retailer buying from US distributors?
questions.guidance: YES if: Has their own product line with their brand name, mentions customs/import operations, overseas suppliers, manufacturing partners. NO if: Stocks many third-party brands from US distributors with no evidence of direct importing. UNCLEAR if: Can't determine sourcing.

questions.question: What specific signals suggest this company imports goods into the US?
What specific signals suggest this company ships internationally?

classification_logic.description: Classification based on import/export signals and business type. Use industry inference when explicit proof unavailable.
classification_logic.internal_test: Did this company likely import goods into the US and then ship those goods out of the US?

sufficiency_check.prompt_addition: Focus on finding: (1) Products page to confirm physical goods, (2) About/Our Story for manufacturing/sourcing info, (3) Shipping page for international shipping, (4) FAQ for additional details. Use industry inference for imports when explicit evidence unavailable.

page_priority.description: Prioritize pages that reveal import/export activity

industry_inference.description: Use these rules to INFER import likelihood based on industry, even without explicit statements.
likely_imports: Apparel/fashion brands (unless explicitly 'Made in USA' with US factory proof)
Eyewear frames (almost always imported, even if lenses made in US)
Specialty tools (German, Japanese, Swiss precision tools)
Furniture (especially if no US factory mentioned)

qualified.reasoning: Apparel brand (industry inference: likely imported), offers international shipping
Footwear company (almost always imported), ships to Canada and Mexico
Consumer electronics (typically imported), international shipping unclear

disqualified.reasoning: Print-on-demand t-shirts - printed in US, no imports
Multi-brand retailer buying from US distributors, not direct importer
Explicitly Made in USA with US factory, US-only shipping

edge_cases.scenario: Retailer that is also the brand owner
edge_cases.handling: QUALIFIED - brand owners typically import directly

edge_cases.scenario: US company with some imported components
edge_cases.handling: QUALIFIED - partial imports still qualify for drawback
Needs investigation - may not be importer of record
